article:
In brief: We're expecting a mix of sun and rain over the next week in Abuja, with temperatures remaining warm to hot, and humidity levels staying relatively high. Today will be a dry and sunny day, but as we head into the rest of the week, our chances for rain increase, particularly from Thursday onwards. If you have outdoor plans, it's a good idea to stay up to date with the latest forecast, especially if you're planning on traveling to or from the city.

As we start the day, the temperature is sitting at 26°C, which feels more like 30°C due to the high humidity. The wind is light, blowing at just 6 km/h, and the UV index is at 0, indicating that it's still quite early in the morning. If you're planning on commuting to work or school, you'll be pleased to know that traffic on the Nnamdi Azikiwe Expressway is currently light, with speeds of 38 km/h, which is the same as the free-flow speed. This should make for a relatively smooth journey to your destination.

Looking out at the city, the skies are clear, and the sun is shining brightly, casting a golden glow over the rooftops of the National Assembly Complex and the surrounding buildings. As we head into the day, we can expect plenty of sunshine, with not a cloud in the sky. This will be a great day to get outside and enjoy the fresh air, whether that's taking a walk around the National Arboretum or simply sitting outside and enjoying a cup of coffee.

### 2026-04-16
Today is looking like it will be a dry and sunny day, with plenty of blue skies and warm temperatures. The high will reach 34°C, while the low will dip down to 26°C. There's only a 15% chance of rain, so it's unlikely that we'll see any precipitation today. If you're planning on traveling to or from the city, the good news is that the weather shouldn't have any impact on road conditions. The roads should be dry and clear, making for a smooth journey.

As we head into the evening, the temperature will start to cool down, and the humidity will remain relatively high. This could lead to some fog formation in low-lying areas, particularly around the Kubwa District. However, this shouldn't have any significant impact on travel or outdoor activities. [MAP: Satellite image of Abuja, showing clear skies and no cloud cover]

### 2026-04-17
Tomorrow is looking like it will be a bit more unsettled, with a 45% chance of rain. The high will reach 35°C, while the low will dip down to 27°C. The increased chance of rain is due to a low-pressure system that's moving into the region, bringing with it a mass of moist air. This could lead to some thunderstorms developing, particularly in the late afternoon and evening.

If you're planning on traveling to the nearby Zuma Rock, you may want to keep an eye on the weather forecast, as the rain could make the roads slippery and hazardous. Additionally, the increased humidity could lead to some mist formation around the rock, reducing visibility. However, the rain could also bring some welcome relief from the heat, and make the scenery look particularly lush and green.

### 2026-04-18
On Thursday, the chance of rain increases to 55%, with the high reaching 31°C and the low dipping down to 27°C. The low-pressure system will be fully entrenched over the region, bringing with it a good chance of precipitation. This could lead to some significant rainfall totals, particularly in the evening and overnight hours.

If you're planning on commuting to or from the city, you may want to leave a bit earlier than usual, as the rain could make the roads slippery and slow. Additionally, the increased humidity could lead to some fog formation, particularly in low-lying areas. However, the rain could also bring some welcome relief from the heat, and make the city feel cooler and more comfortable.

[MAP: Radar image of the low-pressure system, showing a mass of moist air moving into the region]

### 2026-04-19
On Friday, the chance of rain increases to 68%, with the high reaching 33°C and the low dipping down to 26°C. The low-pressure system will still be in place, bringing with it a good chance of precipitation. This could lead to some significant rainfall totals, particularly in the evening and overnight hours.

If you're planning on traveling to the nearby University of Abuja, you may want to keep an eye on the weather forecast, as the rain could make the roads slippery and hazardous. Additionally, the increased humidity could lead to some mist formation around the campus, reducing visibility. However, the rain could also bring some welcome relief from the heat, and make the scenery look particularly lush and green.

### 2026-04-20
On Saturday, the chance of rain decreases to 55%, with the high reaching 33°C and the low dipping down to 26°C. The low-pressure system will be starting to move out of the region, but there's still a good chance of precipitation. This could lead to some scattered thunderstorms, particularly in the late afternoon and evening.

If you're planning on spending the day outdoors, you may want to keep an eye on the weather forecast, as the rain could make things a bit more uncomfortable. However, the decreased chance of rain could also bring some welcome sunshine, and make the city feel cooler and more comfortable. [MAP: Satellite image of Abuja, showing a mix of sun and cloud cover]

### 2026-04-21
On Sunday, the chance of rain decreases to 5%, with the high reaching 36°C and the low dipping down to 26°C. The low-pressure system will be fully out of the region, bringing with it a return to dry and sunny conditions. This will be a great day to get outside and enjoy the fresh air, whether that's taking a walk around the Millennium Park or simply sitting outside and enjoying a cup of coffee.

As we head into the evening, the temperature will start to cool down, and the humidity will remain relatively low. This could lead to some clear skies, and a beautiful sunset over the city. If you're planning on traveling to or from the city, the good news is that the weather shouldn't have any impact on road conditions. The roads should be dry and clear, making for a smooth journey.

### 2026-04-22
On Monday, the chance of rain remains at 5%, with the high reaching 35°C and the low dipping down to 26°C. The dry and sunny conditions will continue, making for a great day to get outside and enjoy the fresh air. This will be a great opportunity to take a walk around the city, and enjoy the scenery, particularly around the National Assembly Complex and the surrounding buildings.

As we head into the rest of the week, it's looking like the dry and sunny conditions will continue, with not much chance of rain. However, it's always a good idea to keep an eye on the weather forecast, as things can change quickly. We'll be keeping a close eye on the situation, and will bring you updates as necessary. [MAP: Satellite image of Abuja, showing clear skies and no cloud cover]
